name: Digestion Process
description: Digestion Process in Animals. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A29[Food Ingestion] --> B29[Mechanical Breakdown]
    B29 --> C29[Enzyme Secretion]
    C29 --> D29[Chemical Breakdown]
    D29 --> E29[Carbohydrate Digestion]
    D29 --> F29[Protein Digestion]
    D29 --> G29[Lipid Digestion]
    E29 --> H29[Absorption]
    F29 --> H29
    G29 --> H29
    H29 --> I29[Transport to Cells]
    I29 --> J29[Metabolic Utilization]
    
    style A29 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B29 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C29 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style D29 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E29 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style F29 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style G29 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style H29 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style I29 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style J29 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Digestion Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: The digestive system: An evolutionary perspective.
Gastrointestinal hormones: their regulation and roles.

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
